XFONT

Applications use the methods of the XFONT interface to rasterize either bitmap or TrueType fonts. For performance reasons, applications should avoid using TrueType fonts, if possible. Support for TrueType fonts is not enabled by default. To enable this support, the title must #define XFONT_TRUETYPE before including xfont.h.

The XFONT interface is obtained by calling one of the functions below:

  • XFONT_OpenBitmapFont
  • XFONT_OpenBitmapFontFromMemory
  • XFONT_OpenDefaultFont
  • XFONT_OpenTrueTypeFont

The methods of the XFONT interface can be organized into the following groups.

Color GetTextColor
SetTextColor
GetBkColor
SetBkColor
GetBkMode
SetBkMode
Rendering TextOut
TextOutToMemory
Size and Positioning GetTextAlignment
SetTextAlignment
GetTextHeight
SetTextHeight
GetIntercharacterSpacing
SetIntercharacterSpacing
Style GetTextStyle
SetTextStyle
Miscellaneous GetClippingRectangle
SetClippingRectangle
GetFontMetrics
GetRLEWidth
SetRLEWidth
GetTextAntialiasLevel
SetTextAntialiasLevel
GetTextExtent

The XFONT interface also supports the AddRef and Release methods. The QueryInterface method is not supported on Xbox.

XFONT does not have any support for TrueType collections.

Requirements

  Header: Declared in xfont.h
  Import Library: Use xgraphics.lib
